summary: Iteration 261 - 4-Tier Economy System Testing Complete. Backend: 49/49 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI components verified including 4 tier cards (Discovery/Resonance/Sovereign/Architect), correct pricing ($0/$27/$49/$89), education levels, discount badges (0%/5%/15%/30%), 4 tabs, Polymath pass ($1,797/year), and Brokerage commission levels (0/6.75/13.5/27%).

issues: Monetization descriptions from backend not displayed in tier cards (features shown instead) - this is by design

critical_code_review_comments: economy.py: SUBSCRIPTION_TIERS dict contains 4 tiers with correct pricing: discovery=$0, resonance=$27, sovereign=$49, architect=$89
economy.py: Each tier has education_level, education_desc, monetization, can_sell, beacon fields
economy.py: Discovery tier has can_sell=false, beacon=false, max_commission_tier=1
economy.py: Architect tier has can_sell=true, beacon=true, max_commission_tier=4
economy.py: GET /discount-rate returns tier discount + failed_trade_charge
economy.py: POST /apply-discount calculates discounted price based on tier
economy.py: COMMISSION_TIERS has 4 levels with rates 0/6.75/13.5/27
economy.py: POST /subscribe creates Stripe checkout for paid tiers, activates directly for Discovery
economy.py: POST /downgrade switches to Discovery tier
economy.py: POLYMATH_PASS price_annual = 1797.00, sets tier to 'architect'
EconomyPage.js: TierCard component renders education_level and education_desc sections
EconomyPage.js: 4-column grid layout for tier cards (grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-4)
EconomyPage.js: Discount badges shown for paid tiers (5%/15%/30% off)
EconomyPage.js: 4 tabs: Subscriptions, Learning Packs, Brokerage, Pack Studio
LearningToggle.js: Floating button at bottom-right with expandable panel
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 261 verified 4-tier economy system recalibration: (1) GET /api/economy/tiers returns 4 tiers: Discovery (Free/Seeker), Resonance ($27/Artisan), Sovereign ($49/Alchemist), Architect ($89/Infrastructure Partner). (2) Each tier has education_level, education_desc, monetization, can_sell, beacon fields. (3) Discovery: can_sell=false, beacon=false. (4) Architect: can_sell=true, beacon=true, max_commission_tier=4. (5) GET /api/economy/discount-rate returns tier discount + failed_trade_charge=0.30. (6) POST /api/economy/apply-discount calculates discounted price. (7) GET /api/economy/commissions returns 4 levels with rates 0/6.75/13.5/27. (8) POST /api/economy/subscribe creates Stripe checkout for paid tiers. (9) POST /api/economy/downgrade switches to Discovery. (10) Frontend: Economy page shows 4 tier cards in grid layout with education sections and discount badges. (11) Frontend: 4 tabs working (Subscriptions/Learning Packs/Brokerage/Pack Studio). (12) Frontend: Polymath All-Access Pass shows $1,797/year. (13) Frontend: Learning Toggle floating button visible. (14) Frontend: Brokerage tab shows all 4 commission levels. (15) data-testids: tier-discovery, tier-resonance, tier-sovereign, tier-architect, select-tier-resonance, select-tier-sovereign, select-tier-architect, tab-subscriptions, tab-packs, tab-commissions, tab-generator, commission-level-1/2/3/4, learning-toggle-btn.
